TextENGNew: The morning of reckoning.
I awoke to the sound of fireworks heralding the start of the festival.
Strength surged through my entire body. When I touched my mouth, I realized I'd been smiling unconsciously.
changeReason: Old says 'couldn't help but laugh' (aware); Japanese means 'smiling unconsciously.'

TextENGNew: Hatred, like a nutrient, had fattened my killing intent -- and it hungered for more.
changeReason: New adds 'and it hungered for more' matching JPN もっと獲物をとせがんでいる (craving more prey), which old omitted.

TextENGNew: I concealed the fangs of a savage predator behind my mask, then threw open the shuttered window.
changeReason: New adds 'behind my mask' and 'threw open the shuttered window' more faithful to JPN 巧みに隠す and 締め切っていた窓を開け放った.

TextENGNew: The sky outside was heavy with towering thunderheads, portending a storm that felt close at hand.
changeReason: New uses 'towering thunderheads' matching JPN 入道雲 (cumulonimbus), while old said generic 'thick clouds.'

TextENGNew: The stage was set.
Perhaps his death cries would be drowned out by the howling storm.
I could hardly wait for the climactic finale.
When I came downstairs, Mom and Dad were leisurely sipping their coffee.
When they saw me, they said good morning, same as always.
And I said good morning right back, same as always.
......That effortless, casual exchange felt so peaceful.
And at the same time... I was keenly aware of the darkness steadily eroding into my heart.
``Time to eat!``
changeReason: Old translates いただきます as 'Thanks for the food!' which is close to the meaning; new's 'Time to eat!' loses the gratitude aspect entirely.

TextENGNew: A warm, harmonious table.
Looking at my parents, so blissfully unaware, made my heart ache.
I'm sorry...
The Keiichi Maebara you raised with such care is already gone.
I'm the one who erased him...
I shoveled the food into my mouth as if to sever the last threads of attachment.
changeReason: New captures JPN 未練を振り切る (sever lingering attachments) more precisely than old's 'take my mind off regrets.'

TextENGNew: I chewed ravenously, as if biting down on my weakness and fear to crush them.
changeReason: New captures JPN 甘えや不安をかみ殺す (bite down to suppress weakness and anxiety) while old simplified, missing 甘え.

TextENGNew: I could feel my own eyes glinting with murderous intent.
I wiped the saliva that had dribbled from my lip, then rose to my feet.
``Thanks for breakfast.@k@vS18/01/160100387. ...I'm heading out.``
Without a moment's hesitation, I headed straight for the door.
Hidden inside the backpack on my back were three folding shovels.
Shovels purchased for family camping trips...
No one could have imagined they'd be used as tools for a murder.
I opened the door.
I looked back, just once.
A farewell to everything my life had been until now.
I steeled myself and took the first step onto the road stretching out before me.
Behind me, the door closed.
The gentle cradle that had always caught me was gone.
Rejection and acceptance -- both one-way streets... never to cross paths again.
Dad... Mom... I'm no longer the person you knew. I'm about to become... a murderer.
And with that, I shut down my thoughts entirely.
The endless spiral of anguish, the useless idealism -- none of it mattered anymore.
All I needed now was to be faithful to my desire.
``Farewell...``
That word was aimed at myself, as well.
I murmured it under my breath, straddled my bicycle, and pushed off.
I tore through the road lined with dense, overgrown trees, pedaling hard toward the garbage dump.
Along the way, I passed a group of children chattering excitedly.
They were probably on their way to the shrine. Their smiles overlapped with the image of Satoko.
The grounds of the Furude Shrine must be adorned in their festive best by now, decorated for the year's grandest celebration.
...Cheerful red-and-white curtains, lanterns strung up in abundance.
...The hum of generators powering the food stalls.
...The voices of children dashing about.
And families smiling as they watch it all. ...Today was the Watanagashi festival.
......Were the others already out there, having fun among the crowd?
What about Satoko?
...Would she be able to forget her painful days, even for a moment... and show us that dazzling smile of hers once more?
My legs drove the pedals with renewed force.
As I gripped the handlebars, I could see the pale blue veins standing out on my hands, the arteries pulsing beneath the skin.
I needed new blood.
A strange sense of omnipotence drove me forward.
As if to bolster my invincible mood and push me higher -- a tailwind blew at my back.
Suddenly, I felt a vivid sensation against my back.
Onward. Onward. Two hands pushing me forward...
Urging me to charge on, all the way to the end of the world...
I made my way through the garbage dump and opened the wagon door -- Rena and Shion were already waiting.
``Good morning, Kei-chan.``
``Morning.``
``Did you sleep well? Did you?``
``Yeah. First good, deep sleep I've had in ages.@k@vS18/01/160100391. Like some kind of spell was finally lifted.``
changeReason: JPN 憑き物が取れた means freed from a possession. Old's 'cast the demons out' implies active exorcism; new's 'spell was finally lifted' is more passive, closer to JPN.

TextENGNew: *Thud.*
The moment the wagon door swung shut, an oppressive tension enclosed us.
An inescapable strain wound tight. We all forgot to breathe, holding the air in our lungs.
Sweat began to seep steadily from every pore.
The stale air filled the sealed space, circulating endlessly with no outlet.
``...Preparations are already complete.``
A single rope lay before Rena and Shion. As planned, it had been painted solid black.
Beside it lay Satoshi's bat.
I pulled the shovels from my backpack and lined them up alongside the other items.
Three tools, gathered to carve open the path to our future.
They exuded a grave, weighty presence -- like three sacred implements prepared for a solemn ritual.
A faint sound rang out, reminiscent of rusted metal.
My body, hypersensitive from the tension, flinched hard.
``Did anyone... hear that just now...?``
Rena and Shion tilted their heads and exchanged glances.
Was I hearing things...?
I swept my gaze around the interior of the car. Then... behind Rena, I spotted a large black duffel bag, placed as though deliberately hidden.
``What's inside that bag?``
``I heard a sound coming from inside that bag.``
``What are you talking about...?@k@vS18/02/160200253. There wasn't any sound.@k@vS18/02/160200254. You must be hearing things, Keiichi-kun.``
``Then open it and show me.``
``...Keiichi-kun, you're kind of scaring me...``
``I said show me!``
``What's gotten into you...?@k@vS18/06/160600246. You're frightening Rena-san...``
``I'm telling you I heard something!``
``There was no sound!``
Shion stepped in front of Rena as if to shield her.
``Move, Shion.``
``No.@k@vS18/06/160600249. Something's wrong with you, Kei-chan.@k@vS18/06/160600250. With that look on your face...``
Rena and Shion huddled together, pulling away from me. They looked at me as if staring at something terrifying...
``What's with you two...?``
A trail of sweat ran down my back. As if expelled by my own inner pressure, what I felt on my skin was coldness, and a tremor.
I slowly reached out and gripped Satoshi's bat.
``Open it...``
Rena's eyes glistened with a fear she could no longer contain. But I pressed her further to open the bag.
With undisguised anxiety on her face, Rena slowly unzipped the duffel bag.
I swallowed hard.
What emerged from inside was... a five-tiered stack of lacquered food boxes.
``The Rena Special, huh...``
Every ounce of strength drained from my body at once, and the recoil nearly knocked me over backward.
``B-because...@k@vS18/02/160200258. if we're going to dig a really big hole, I figured we'd get hungry, so...``
``Then why didn't you just show me right away?@k@vS18/01/160100403. You gave me a cold sweat for nothing...``
``...I'm sorry, Keiichi-kun.``
``No, I'm the one who should apologize for yelling.``
Rena smiled brightly, her expression one of relief.
I smiled back, but inside, conflicting thoughts were churning...
That sound from earlier... It definitely wasn't the zipper.
It was a far more imposing sound... one that radiated destructive force... one that pierced through my skull.
It felt like some kind of omen, but my mind slid away from it quickly, refusing to probe deeper.
changeReason: Old adds 'conscience telling me to turn back'; new correctly says mind slid away from the thought.

TextENGNew: Perhaps I was simply too afraid to face it head-on, and wanted to plug my ears and let it pass...
I reviewed our procedures one more time while packing the tools into my backpack.
Somewhere far off, I could hear the buzzing of cicadas. We looked at each other's faces, and then, without anyone saying a word, we stood.
``Let's go.``
We crawled out of the wagon in silence and climbed the garbage heap, testing our footing with every step.
*Rustle*... *Rustle*... *Crash!*
I looked back.
The station wagon, balanced precariously on its unstable footing, was swaying slightly.
A harbinger of collapse...
As if to shake off the ominous premonition, we scrambled through the garbage dump at a desperate pace.
The spot we'd chosen to bury the body lay deep in a dense, dark forest, far past the thicket and deeper still beyond.
It was uncharted territory -- the kind of place that seemed to reject human intrusion.
Making it all the way from the dump without a single person spotting us felt like a miraculous stroke of luck.
When I looked up, thick, dark clouds were concealing our presence from above.
Good... even the heavens were on our side!
``Let's make it here.``
I drove a shovel into the spot Shion pointed to.
``It looks like a grave marker...``
``For that man, yes.@k@vS18/06/160600254. But for us, it might just be a monument to our victory.``
Tonight, one man would meet his final day.
And from then on, we would celebrate this day every year.
The proud anniversary of the day we made the right decision.
``Let's dig...``
I tightened my grip and drove the shovel into the earth, again and again.
Shion and Rena followed suit without a word.
Single-mindedly, driven by hatred, we turned the soil.
*Thud. Thud. Thud.*
Shovels stabbing into the ground at steep angles.
*Crunch. Crunch. Crunch.*
Bone-dry earth, gouged out with insatiable hunger.
Rena suddenly let out a soft, gentle laugh.
``You know, around this same time last year...``
Rena's gaze drifted somewhere far away.
``Rena ran into Satoko-chan at the garbage dump.@k@vS18/02/160200264. I was so lonely and scared at the time, crying all by myself...``
Rena gave a bashful shrug, then wiped the sweat from her brow.
``I smiled and told her it was nothing, but Satoko-chan looked so worried... and she started crying.@k@vS18/02/160200267. She said, 'If Rena-san is crying, then I'll cry with you!'``
``As we cried together, she told me she knew what it was like -- how once you start crying alone, the tears never stop...@k@vS18/02/160200269. Then Satoko-chan took my hand and wouldn't let go.``
A faint smile crossed Rena's face.
``She was so kyute and sweet that my tears just stopped on their own.@k@vS18/02/160200271. And then something warm welled up inside me, and we both started laughing.``
``The two of us shouting 'Ahahahahaha~!' at the top of our lungs, on and on without getting tired...@k@vS18/02/160200274. I just remembered that...``
``That sounds just like Satoko...``
For a fleeting instant, a shadow passed over Shion's face.
``Even back then, Satoko-chan was suffering so much more than Rena...``
Remembering how powerless I had been, I placed my hand on Rena's lowered shoulder.
``The past is the past.@k@vS18/01/160100410. What matters is now.@k@vS18/01/160100411. And for the sake of the future, we're here doing this.``
``You're right...``
Rena let out a shy 'ehehe,' then took up her shovel again.
*Thud. Thud. Thud.*
*Crunch. Crunch. Crunch.*
We dug on relentlessly.
The mounds of excavated earth grew higher.
In inverse proportion, the hole before us gaped wider and wider, its maw threatening to swallow everything whole.
changeReason: Old misses the key JPN imagery of 巨大な口をぱっくり開き (gaping maw); new captures the hole threatening to swallow everything.

TextENGNew: Peering into the bottom of the hole was like staring into a pitch-black abyss. Like a black hole.
If I didn't keep my grip on sanity, I felt I might be sucked in.
I drew a deep breath, wiped the sweat away, and repositioned the shovel.
*Thud. Thud. Thud.*
Beads of sweat streamed down my forehead.
*Crunch. Crunch. Crunch.*
A dull ache throbbed through my biceps.
Threading through the monotonous rhythm, a refreshing breeze swept over our heads.
Shion exhaled softly and looked up at the sky.
Her gaze darted around, scanning the surroundings.
``Shii-chan...?``
Rena and I stopped digging and looked up as well.
``Just now, I felt like... someone stroked my head...``
After saying that, Shion closed her eyes, her expression rigid. And then--
Amid her sobs, a hoarse voice echoed through the clearing.
``...Shii-chan?``
``...Uuuuuu...@k@vS18/06/160600264. Aaaah, aaaaaaaaaah----!!``
Without warning, Shion erupted into a scream that was barely human.
changeReason: Old says 'began to scream'; Japanese 声にならない悲鳴 means a scream that couldn't form into words.

TextENGNew: She thrashed her head wildly, then clamped both hands over her mouth, her whole body trembling.
changeReason: Old says 'scratched at her hair'; Japanese means wildly thrashing/shaking her hair.

TextENGNew: ``Shion!@k@vS18/01/160100413. What's wrong?!``
I threw down my shovel and caught her by the shoulders.
But Shion pushed me aside, mumbling incoherently, and crumpled to the ground.
``...I'm definitely going to be killed...``
What?
``...The curse of Oyashiro-sama... divine punishment is going to fall on me...@k@vS18/06/160600267. That is the ending prepared for me...@k@vS18/06/160600268. I know it...``
``Shion, what's gotten into you?@k@vS18/01/160100416. You won't be killed.@k@vS18/01/160100417. I won't let anyone kill you!``
changeReason: Old says 'Teppei is the one who's gonna die!' but JPN says 殺されるのは鉄平なんだ, and new says 'I won't let anyone kill you!' -- both diverge from JPN but in different ways. New drops Teppei reference entirely.

TextENGNew: ``That's right.@k@vS18/02/160200280. The curse of Oyashiro-sama would never come for you, Shii-chan...``
``I'm not the sort of person everyone thinks I am...@k@vS18/06/160600270. I'm... terrible... a terrible person...!``
Shion's eyes were vacant, her expression hollow.
``Stop it, Shion.@k@vS18/01/160100419. You're not a sinner!@k@vS18/01/160100420. You're doing the right thing!``
Shion shook her head in slow, deliberate movements.
``No... I'm the worst...@k@vS18/06/160600272. All this time, I've been even more terrible than Sis... I've done such terrible things...!``
``What... are you saying, Shii-chan...?``
``The truth is... I've always hated Satoko...``
She squeezed the words out as if wringing them from her body, and a single tear traced down her cheek.

``Always clinging to Satoshi-kun, always leaning on him, depending on him...@k@vS18/06/160600275. Receiving all of Satoshi-kun's love as if it were her birthright...@k@vS18/06/160600276. I was jealous of her...``
``If I could become Satoko, I could be by Satoshi-kun's side forever...@k@vS18/06/160600278. The more I fell in love with Satoshi-kun, the more I envied Satoko... and the more I hated her...``
changeReason: Old says 'Satoko was always able to stay by his side'; Japanese says 'If I could become Satoko' -- conditional wish, not fact.

TextENGNew: Large tears spilled from Shion's eyes.
She swiped roughly at her eyes with dirt-caked hands, then collapsed forward, both palms hitting the ground.
``In my heart, I wished...@k@vS18/06/160600280. that if Oyashiro-sama truly existed...@k@vS18/06/160600281. please make Satoko unhappy...``
``...Shion, you...``
``...So Oyashiro-sama really does exist, doesn't it...?@k@vS18/06/160600283. ...Because Satoko's greatest misfortune was Satoshi-kun disappearing...``
``Even if we can't see it, it never misses a single whisper we make.@k@vS18/06/160600285. It sees into the depths of our hearts, and it never overlooks anything...``
``...That's right.@k@vS18/02/160200283. Oyashiro-sama *exists*.``
Rena murmured it quietly, but with unshakable conviction.
``Watching over us, even now...``
Silence fell.
We stood rooted to the spot as if paralyzed.
As if to mock us, the cicadas shrieked all around.
A gust of wind stirred the weeds at our feet, making them rustle.
Something shadowy seemed to close in on us... with the agility and suppleness of a whip... like countless quick, darting footsteps...
A nameless, alien sensation invaded my body, racing through every nerve.
Like something parasitic had latched onto me, gnawing away at my insides bit by bit.
A fierce thirst seized my throat.
An irresistible urge to claw at my own skin gripped me.
Was this a hallucination, crystallizing inside a body consumed by paranoia...?
*Thud. Thud. Thud.*
*Crunch. Crunch. Crunch.*
Desperate to shake off the fear and anxiety writhing inside me, I threw myself back into digging.
``...Sorry, but I don't believe in any supernatural nonsense!@k@vS18/01/160100424. I chose to believe in you two.@k@vS18/01/160100425. And in Satoko!``
changeReason: New drops two full lines from the JPN: 沙都子をこの手で救い出す and もうそれしか頭ん中にはない (saving Satoko with my own hands / that's all I can think about).

TextENGNew: If my resolve wavered now, my heart would shatter into pieces.
I was terrified of what would happen if my hands stopped moving.
After watching me in silence for a time, Shion slowly rose to her feet.
She picked up her shovel, stood beside me, and began turning the earth once more.
``Shion...``
*Thud. Thud. Thud. Thud. Thud. Thud.*
*Crunch. Crunch. Crunch. Crunch. Crunch. Crunch.*
``You woke me up, Kei-chan.@k@vS18/06/160600287. I think the reason I wanted to save Satoko was partly to atone for what I felt toward Satoshi-kun... I tried to become Satoko... to take her place.``

``But I think that was wrong.@k@vS18/06/160600289. I didn't want to become Satoko.@k@vS18/06/160600290. I wanted to become Satoshi-kun.@k@vS18/06/160600291. So I wanted to become like Satoshi-kun and save Satoko!``
Shion smiled at me through a face caked in mud.
Her expression held the clarity of someone who had laid bare everything inside.
We always fill the silences with meaningless chatter instead of saying what we really mean.
If we ever spoke our true feelings aloud, we feared our very being would dry out and crumble... That was the damp, creeping madness we were all running from.
*Thud. Thud. Thud.*
*Crunch. Crunch. Crunch.*
As if fleeing their gazes, I jumped down into the hole and began digging from the inside.
If I stopped digging, the tears would fall...
I couldn't afford to be swept away by something as lukewarm as sentimentality right now.
*Thud. Thud. Thud.*
*Crunch. Crunch. Crunch.*
``I'm the same.@k@vS18/01/160100429. That's why I have to save Satoko.@k@vS18/01/160100430. To save her as Keiichi Maebara -- as myself.``
changeReason: New drops JPN references to Satoshi and the idea of overcoming resentment, replacing with 'To save her as Keiichi Maebara -- as myself' which alters the motivation.

TextENGNew: *Thud. Thud. Thud. Thud. Thud. Thud. Thud. Thud. Thud.*
*Crunch. Crunch. Crunch. Crunch. Crunch. Crunch. Crunch. Crunch. Crunch.*
As if drawn by my resolve, Rena and Shion both climbed down into the hole and began to dig alongside me.
*Thud. Thud. Thud. Thud. Thud. Thud. Thud. Thud. Thud.*
*Crunch. Crunch. Crunch. Crunch. Crunch. Crunch. Crunch. Crunch. Crunch.*
After that, not one of us spoke another word.
Every mouth was set in a thin, hard line. Sweat poured freely, unwiped. We surrendered ourselves entirely to the repetitive act of digging.
Each of us harbored secret thoughts in our hearts. Forbidden, wicked things.
We were all aware of it, yet none of us dared ask. We didn't even ask ourselves.
Right now, we couldn't afford the slightest crack in our righteous cause of 'saving a friend.'
Confessing might shake our will to kill...
Isn't that right?
Every human being, if you dig deep enough, is rotten somewhere inside.
Some are lucky enough to live and die in peace, never catching a whiff of their own decay.
But for those unlucky enough to become aware -- or fated to become aware -- a hell of spreading rot awaits.
How ironic.
The harder you try to hide it, the wider the wound gapes instead of closing. Just like this hole...
And someday, perhaps, we'd rot away entirely...
Before I knew it, the sun was beginning to set. Piercing through the thick clouds, the evening light poured down on us.
I looked up at the sky from the dim bottom of the hole.
``...Think this is enough...?``
At my signal, we climbed out of the earth.
``We really dug quite a lot...``
I tossed the shovel aside, crouched down, and peered into the hole.
The shape was irregular since we'd dug from three sides, but it was deeper than we'd planned.
It was more than large enough to bury a single body.
``...Haah... haah... haah...``
My ragged breathing seemed to be swallowed into the depths of the hole.
*Clang*... *scrape scrape*... *thud.*
The shovel slipped from Rena's exhausted grip and tumbled into the hole.
``Oh... what do I do...?@k@vS18/02/160200286. Rena zoned out for a moment...``
Rena stretched her arm as far as she could, trying to reach the shovel.
But it was far too deep -- the shovel was swallowed by darkness, its shape barely visible.
``Can't be helped.@k@vS18/01/160100436. I'll jump down and grab it.``
``Right.@k@vS18/06/160600293. We can't leave any evidence behind.``
``Sorry, Keiichi-kun...``
``Don't worry about it.``
I placed my hands on the edge of the hole and leaped down.
*Thump.*
I botched the landing and toppled over inside.
The taste of dirt filled my mouth. I spat frantically and felt around blindly for the shovel.
Almost immediately, my fingertips struck cold metal.
I grabbed the shovel and stood up, ready to climb out.
That was when...
*Rrring*...
I heard it again -- 'that sound' from inside the wagon.
And then...
......I could hear them...!
Rena and Shion were... deliberately speaking in voices too low for me to hear...!
I strained my ears.
Focusing every sense I had into my hearing, I strained to catch their conversation, determined not to miss a single word.
......'Keiichi'... my name... 'mistake'... 'kill'......?!
What? What were the two of them discussing...?!
......'Doesn't know'...... 'pathetic'... What did that mean...?@k Were they talking about me...?@k Wait, what...?@k ...'From the beginning'......?!
What is this...?@k Those two, could they possibly... no, from the very start, have they been...?!
Unable to hold it in, a groan escaped my lips.
Doesn't know anything...?
Going to die anyway...?
Their opaque conversation... reeked of something sinister.
Unable to fathom their true intentions, my consciousness began to slip away.
Was a separate plan being hatched behind my back...?
Was I not allowed to be part of this other plan...? Or was I already being turned as one of its gears...?
Question marks crawled across my mind, blocking rational thought.
Desperate to hold onto my sanity, I took deep breath after deep breath.
Where on earth was I...?
Where on earth was I going...? Where was I being led...?
Without warning, the setting sun was blotted out by two shadows.
``Keiichi-kun, did you find it?``
I raised my head cautiously -- and there were Rena and Shion, looking down at me with innocent smiles.
``What's wrong?@k@vS18/06/160600297. ...You look awfully pale...``
``Keiichi-kun,@k@vS18/02/160200292. here, grab Rena's hand.``
Rena reached down toward me with a worried expression. But I hesitated for an instant -- was it truly safe to take her hand...?
Was that a hallucination...?
It had to be. Just a hallucination.
There was no way Rena and Shion would betray me!
Was it really a hallucination, though...?
Stop it! Stop trying to tear my mind apart! Don't listen!
This plan had been plagued by discord from the start.
The baffling questions. Those two eyes. The shadows that followed. The silent phone calls. The hidden agendas. And Oyashiro-sama's two hands...
Every action I took unraveled another thread. I had been desperately pretending not to see.
That's right -- it has to be a hallucination!
It was the last scrap of guilt still inside me, trying to hold me back by making me hear things.
This was my punishment. The punishment dealt to me for abandoning my humanity.
``Keiichi-kun...?``
I forced a smile up at Rena as she peered down at me with anxious eyes, then clutched her hand as if clinging to it for dear life.
``Ready? One, two, heave!``
Pulled by Rena's hands, my body floated upward.
Everything before my eyes seemed to flash past like images from a dying man's life.
When I looked up, the towering thunderheads that had blanketed the sky had vanished without a trace.
The storm I had been counting on was not going to come.
Had the scenario I'd written been rewritten without my knowing?
Was I nothing but a puppet, forced to dance according to a script penned by someone else...?
``It's almost time...``
Perhaps sensing my unease, Shion prompted us with a reminder of the deadline.
I couldn't think anymore.
I was exhausted.
All I could do now was follow Rena and Shion as they walked ahead.
Without knowing what awaited us at the end...
With only the knowledge that tonight, it would all be over...
